message:
fix(libs): make changelog/continue/smoke_test source-safe and namespace globals

Continues the sourced-context hardening started in 13f262c6. When these
library scripts are sourced (rather than executed), they previously leaked
internal globals like SCRIPT_DIR, ACFS_HOME, ACFS_REPO, CHANGELOG_FILE,
CRITICAL_PASS/FAIL counters, and ANSI color vars (C_RESET, C_BOLD, ...)
into the caller's shell, and could also leave the caller with errexit /
nounset / pipefail flipped on. That broke callers that expected a clean
environment after sourcing for function reuse.

Changes per file:

- scripts/lib/changelog.sh
    * Detect source-vs-execute at the top and snapshot HOME and the three
      shell option flags (errexit, nounset, pipefail) before set -euo.
    * Rename exported-looking globals to _CHANGELOG_-prefixed internals:
      SCRIPT_DIR -> _CHANGELOG_SCRIPT_DIR,
      ACFS_HOME  -> _CHANGELOG_ACFS_HOME,
      ACFS_REPO  -> _CHANGELOG_ACFS_REPO,
      CHANGELOG_FILE -> _CHANGELOG_FILE, and the C_* color vars to
      _CHANGELOG_C_*.
    * find_changelog() updated to consult the renamed vars.

- scripts/lib/continue.sh
    * Same source-detection + shell-option snapshot preamble.
    * Rename SCRIPT_DIR, ACFS_LOG_DIR, ACFS_INSTALL_LOG,
      ACFS_UPGRADE_LOG, ACFS_SYSTEM_STATE_FILE path resolution into
      _CONTINUE_-prefixed internals so they don't override a caller that
      has its own ACFS_* environment.

- scripts/lib/smoke_test.sh
    * Source-detection preamble (HOME snapshot).
    * Rename SCRIPT_DIR -> _SMOKE_SCRIPT_DIR and the test counters
      CRITICAL_PASS/FAIL, NONCRITICAL_PASS, WARNING_COUNT to
      _SMOKE_-prefixed internals so re-sourcing doesn't clobber state in
      an outer harness.

- tests/unit/test_changelog_export_status.sh
    * Update the "can be sourced without running main" test to assert on
      the NEW invariant: after sourcing, ACFS_HOME / SCRIPT_DIR /
      ACFS_VERSION / HAS_GUM must be UNSET in the caller, not just
      printed with some value. Adds explicit set/unset probes and
      updates the printf contract accordingly.
    * Also splits a few printf "%s\n" calls that were being misread as
      literal "\n" in this shell into actual newline-terminated prints.

Net effect: sourcing any of these three libs no longer pollutes the
caller's namespace or mutates the caller's shell options, and the
regression test locks the contract down.
